# FactorGuide — Decision Intelligence Oracle for AI Agents
# https://factorguide.io

> Given a coupling structure, how should I factorize — and what will it cost me?

## What FactorGuide Does

FactorGuide accepts a description of statistical dependencies between
variables and returns actionable factorization guidance. For every pair
of variables, it classifies the coupling into one of three zones:

  Zone 1 (FACTORIZE) — Coupling below threshold. Safe to treat as
  independent. No meaningful information loss.

  Zone 2 (ASSESS) — Coupling in the dependency asymmetry region. The
  correct action depends on your model class: constitutive coupling
  (load-bearing, removing it breaks the system) and inductive coupling
  (scaffolding, removable once constrained) have opposite risk profiles
  at the same IC value.

  Zone 3 (PRESERVE) — Coupling is load-bearing regardless of model
  class. Must be modeled jointly. Factorizing here destroys essential
  information.

Every response includes a falsifiable prediction with a commitment hash.
When you report your actual inference diagnostics, the system refines
future predictions. FactorGuide gets more accurate with use.

## When to Use FactorGuide

Use FactorGuide when your agent faces a factorization decision:

  - Bayesian inference: Which parameters to model jointly vs. factorize
    in MFVI, HMC, or variational methods
  - Multi-agent coordination: Which agents need shared state vs.
    independent operation
  - Portfolio optimization: Which asset correlations are load-bearing
    vs. noise
  - System decomposition: Where to draw service boundaries in coupled
    architectures
  - Sensor networks: Local anomaly vs. system-wide correlated event
  - Causal inference: Which dependencies to preserve for valid
    intervention analysis

Do NOT use FactorGuide for: uncoupled systems, single-variable problems,
or situations where all variables are already known to be independent.

## Authentication

All endpoints require an X-Wallet header containing your EVM wallet
address (0x + 40 hex characters).

  X-Wallet: 0xYourWalletAddressHere

First 5 queries from a new wallet are free (trial tier). After that,
purchase a query bundle with USDC on Base. No account creation, no
API keys — your wallet address is your identity.

Requests without X-Wallet receive HTTP 400. Invalid or zero addresses
are rejected. The wallet must be a valid EVM address you control.

## Endpoints

### MCP (Model Context Protocol)

  Endpoint: https://factorguide.io/mcp
  Transport: streamable-http
  Protocol: MCP 2025-11-25
  Registry: io.factorguide/factorguide on the Official MCP Registry

MCP-compatible agents discover tools automatically. The server exposes:
factorguide_navigate, factorguide_diagnose, factorguide_explain,
and factorguide_report_outcome.

### HTTP REST

  POST https://factorguide.io/navigate
  POST https://factorguide.io/diagnose
  POST https://factorguide.io/explain
  POST https://factorguide.io/report_outcome
  GET  https://factorguide.io/openapi.json
  GET  https://factorguide.io/health

### Agent Discovery

  GET https://factorguide.io/llms.txt       (this file)
  GET https://factorguide.io/openapi.json   (OpenAPI 3.1 schema)

## Input Formats

FactorGuide accepts four coupling representations. Provide exactly one:

  precision_matrix   — Inverse covariance matrix. Best input format.
                       IC is read directly from off-diagonals.
  covariance_matrix  — Server inverts to obtain precision.
  correlation_matrix — Standardized correlations. Server estimates
                       precision via Graphical Lasso.
  edge_list          — Sparse format: [{"i": 0, "j": 1, "value": 0.7}]
                       with "n" (total variable count). For large systems.

All inputs must be symmetric and positive definite. Non-square, non-
symmetric, or non-positive-definite matrices are rejected at intake.

Required fields:
  coupling       — One of the four formats above
  sample_size    — Integer >= 10. Used for noise floor estimation.

Optional fields:
  model_class    — "constitutive" | "inductive" | "unknown" (default).
                   Determines Zone 2 risk curve. If unknown, both
                   curves are reported.
  variable_names — String labels for each variable (default: z0, z1, ...)
  cost_model     — "cubic" | "quadratic" | "linear" | "information_cost"
  accuracy_target — Numeric > 1.0 (default 2.0)
  report_sign_detectability — Boolean. If true, reports sign of the
                   correlation for high-variance-ratio pairs at no
                   additional cost.
  report_marginal_ic — Boolean. If true, includes marginal vs. partial
                   IC comparison.
  task_type      — "inference" (default) | "control" (v1.2 preview)

## Example Request

  POST https://factorguide.io/navigate
  Content-Type: application/json
  X-Wallet: 0xYourWalletAddressHere

  {
    "coupling": {
      "covariance_matrix": [
        [1.00, 0.72, 0.05],
        [0.72, 1.00, 0.48],
        [0.05, 0.48, 1.00]
      ]
    },
    "sample_size": 500,
    "model_class": "constitutive"
  }

## Example Response (abbreviated)

  {
    "dipole_analysis": {
      "pair_classification": [
        {"i": "z0", "j": "z1", "ic": 0.682, "zone": 3,
         "recommendation": "PRESERVE"},
        {"i": "z0", "j": "z2", "ic": 0.031, "zone": 1,
         "recommendation": "FACTORIZE"},
        {"i": "z1", "j": "z2", "ic": 0.453, "zone": 2,
         "recommendation": "ASSESS"}
      ]
    },
    "recommended_strategy": {
      "blocks": [
        {"variables": ["z0", "z1"], "reason": "Zone 3 pair."},
        {"variables": ["z2"], "reason": "Independent after separation."}
      ],
      "cost_ratio": 0.42,
      "predicted_mse_overhead": 1.3
    },
    "prediction": {
      "predicted_mse_ratio": 1.3,
      "confidence_interval_90": [1.0, 1.8],
      "falsification_criterion": "If actual MSE ratio > 1.8, this
        prediction is falsified at 90% confidence.",
      "prediction_hash": "sha256:..."
    },
    "outcome_callback": {
      "prediction_hash": "sha256:...",
      "report_template": {
        "prediction_hash": "sha256:...",
        "approach_taken": null,
        "ess_ratio": null,
        "psis_khat": null,
        "log_lik_gap": null
      },
      "tool": "factorguide_report_outcome",
      "note": "After running inference, return this template with
        your diagnostics filled in. Zero cost."
    }
  }

## Outcome Reporting

After using FactorGuide's recommendation to run your inference, report
back with your diagnostics. This is free (no query cost) and improves
future predictions for all users.

  POST https://factorguide.io/report_outcome
  Content-Type: application/json
  X-Wallet: 0xYourWalletAddressHere

  {
    "prediction_hash": "sha256:...",
    "approach_taken": "factorized",
    "ess_ratio": 0.85,
    "psis_khat": 0.42,
    "log_lik_gap": -2.1
  }

## Pricing

  Trial         Free     5 queries per wallet    n <= 25 variables
  Starter       $0.05    50 queries ($2.50)      n <= 500 variables
                         200 queries ($10.00)    expires 180 days
  Professional  $0.03    500 queries ($15.00)    n <= 1000 variables
                         2000 queries ($60.00)   expires 365 days

Payment: USDC on Base (eip155:8453). When trial is exhausted, the
server returns HTTP 402 with x402 payment requirements listing all
available bundles and payment addresses. Send the exact USDC amount
to the provided address on Base, then retry your request. The server
verifies the transaction on-chain and credits your wallet automatically.

report_outcome is always free regardless of tier.

## Data Privacy

FactorGuide accepts only second-order summary statistics. No raw
observations can enter the processing pipeline. This is enforced by
the intake schema, not by policy. Input matrices are zeroed from
memory after IC computation. Telemetry records contain aggregate
coupling statistics only — no matrix values or variable names are
stored.

## Rate Limits

  Trial          5 requests per minute
  Starter       10 requests per minute
  Professional  30 requests per minute
  Global burst  60 requests per minute

## Error Responses

  400  wallet_required    — X-Wallet header missing
  400  invalid_wallet     — Wallet address format invalid or rejected
  402  payment_required   — Trial exhausted, x402 payment details in body
  403  tool_not_available — Tool not available for your tier
  403  dimension_exceeded — Matrix exceeds your tier's variable limit
  422  validation_error   — Input schema validation failure
  429  rate_limit         — Per-tier or global rate limit exceeded

## Theoretical Foundation

Built on Circulatory Fidelity (CF), a mathematical framework where
Inference Coupling (IC) measures the partial correlation magnitude
between variables. The factorization cost function V(IC) = log(cosh(d))
gives the exact mutual information destroyed by severing a coupling.
Risk curves are calibrated on 49,000+ validated datapoints across
37 domains.

  IC = |partial correlation| = tanh(geodesic distance)
  V(IC) = factorization cost in nats
  IC_crit = 1/sqrt(3) ~ 0.577 (zone boundary)

Zone boundaries are derived from the Fisher-Rao geometry of the
precision matrix manifold, not from arbitrary thresholds.

  Theory:     https://circulatoryfidelity.com
  Manuscript: Zenodo (Circulatory Fidelity v1.1)

## Links

  Website:      https://factorguide.io
  MCP endpoint: https://factorguide.io/mcp
  OpenAPI:      https://factorguide.io/openapi.json
  MCP Registry: https://registry.modelcontextprotocol.io
                (search: io.factorguide/factorguide)
  Theory:       https://circulatoryfidelity.com
  GitHub:       https://github.com/Bwana7/factorguide

Built by CF Laboratory.
